meaning: n.揶揄,戏弄,逗惹vt.取笑,逗恼,奚落,欺负,嘲弄,取笑,强求,梳理
examples: It's one thing to tease your sister, but it's another to hit her.
They tease him; the bubble bursts!
Other children often tease him because of his long hair.
It is not an easy task to tease a national psyche out of a landscape, a geological fact.
But he said it's still tough to tease apart that segment of the family tree.
